item.title: Army veteran sets off fireworks, starts fire outside NYC federal building, FBI says
item.description: A man was arrested after law enforcement officials said he ignited fireworks, poured flammable liquid and started a fire at 26 Federal Plaza in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Pentagon ID's 2 soldiers killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: World Cup champs greeted as heroes in Spain as 2026 tournament comes to an end
item.description: The celebration in Spain continued Monday as World Cup champions La Roja returned home to a heroes' welcome. Nicole Valdes has one last look at the 2026 tournament.

item.title: Video appears to show United employee threaten to call ICE on customer
item.description: A United Airlines employee appears to have been caught on video threatening to call U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on a passenger last week at San Francisco International Airport.

item.title: Tate brothers detained as they face new charges, possible U.K. extradition
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the U.K., where they face rape and sex trafficking charges.

item.title: U.S. carries out 10th night of strikes on Iran after 3 U.S. troops killed
item.description: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

item.title: U.S. gas prices back up to average of $4 a gallon, AAA says
item.description: As fighting with Iran ramps up, a gallon of regular gas is once again $4 on average, up 13 cents in one week, according to AAA. Severin Borenstein, professor at UC Berkeley's Haas School of Business, joins with analysis.

item.title: Troy Jackson set to become Democratic Maine Senate nominee, replacing Platner
item.description: Former state senator Troy Jackson, who calls himself a "pickup progressive," is set to become Maine's Democratic Senate nominee. He'll replace Graham Platner, who left the race following accusations of sexual assault. Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: New round of strikes targeting Iran underway, U.S. officials say
item.description: A 10th straight night of U.S. strikes on Iran is underway as President Trump vows Tehran will pay for the deaths of three American soldiers over the weekend. CBS News senior national security correspondent Charlie D'Agata reports. Then, global affairs analyst Jonathan Wachtel joins to discuss the state of the war.

item.title: Here's how the UK went through 6 prime ministers in 7 years
item.description: Andy Burnham became the UK's new prime minister on Monday, calling for a national "reset" as many Britons struggle with poverty and an elevated cost of living. CBS News' senior foreign correspondent Imtiaz Tyab reports from London and Lindsey Reiser looks at the timeline of recent British prime ministers.

item.title: Fire captain, wife, 3 sons found dead after flash flood in Utah
item.description: The victims were a captain in the Provo, Utah, fire department, his wife and their sons, according to fire officials.

item.title: 5 drown after several enter Ohio's Scioto River to rescue struggling swimmer
item.description: Five people drowned in Ohio's Scioto River after several of them went into the water to help another person who was struggling to swim, authorities said Monday. A motorist called 911 after spotting a frightened child who had been with the group and was running on a nearby road.

item.title: Tate brothers to fight UK extradition over rape, sex trafficking charges
item.description: Brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared in court in Miami on Monday as they fight an extradition request from the UK over new sex crime-related charges. Cristian Benavides reports from Miami.

item.title: Epidemiologist explains how cyclospora test on lettuce sample might have resulted in false positive
item.description: Epidemiologist Ben Miller joins "The Daily Report" to explain how the FDA's testing of a sample of Taylor Farms' lettuce could have resulted in a false positive for cyclospora, as the FDA said it did on Sunday. The FDA said Monday that its investigation and data "continue to converge on shredded iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms locations in central Mexico."

item.title: Gas prices jump across U.S. as conflict with Iran escalates
item.description: AAA says the national average for a gallon of gas has jumped to $4 once again amid recent escalations in the U.S.' war with Iran, causing many Americans to struggle. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Lumberjacks and lumberjills compete in annual world championships
item.description: Far from the world's biggest soccer stage this weekend, thousands watched with no less passion what's known as "the Olympics of the forest." Tony Dokoupil reports.

item.title: Man flipped by bison speaks out: "They move faster than you could ever imagine"
item.description: The 65-year-old man who was flipped eight feet in the air by a bison this month at Yellowstone National Park is sharing his story from his hospital bed. Carl Isom-McDaniel broke his femur in four places, had surgery and is now recovering at a Montana hospital.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha targeting the Gulf Coast
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is barreling down on the Gulf Coast. Lana Zak reports and Lonnie Quinn breaks down the forecast.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha forms, threatening Gulf Coast. See forecast path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Trump to impose 50% tariffs on Canadian hockey sticks, alcohol, other goods
item.description: President Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on a range of Canadian goods, from hockey equipment to alcoholic beverages, escalating the trade dispute between the two countries.

item.title: Vice President Vance and second lady Usha welcome 4th child
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance announced the birth of their fourth child on Sunday. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has the latest.

item.title: 7/20: CBS Evening News
item.description: Suspect arrested in New York City federal building explosion; FDA says it's still investigating Taylor Farms for cyclospora after false positive test.

item.title: Lindsey Graham's sister, Darline, announces bid for full Senate term in South Carolina
item.description: Darline Graham, the sister of late Sen. Lindsey Graham, says she will run for a full Senate term in South Carolina. CBS News political director Fin Gómez has more.

item.title: Qatari-gifted Air Force One to be sent off for defensive upgrades, Trump says
item.description: President Trump acknowledged that the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One jet needs security upgrades, saying the process should take about a month to complete. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: 7/20: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: More U.S. strikes against Iran; two soldiers killed in Iranian strikes identified.

item.title: 5 drown in river in Ohio as child runs for help
item.description: Authorities say five people died near Columbus, Ohio, after several of them went into the water to assist another person who was struggling to swim.

item.title: Gas prices top $4 a gallon again as U.S.-Iran conflict escalates
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gas reached $4.003 on Monday, the first time in over a month it has crossed that threshold, AAA data shows.

item.title: How 401(k) fees can cost tens of thousands in retirement savings
item.description: About four in 10 workers aren't even aware they pay fees on their 401(k)s, research has found. Here's what to know.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, says she'll run for full Senate term
item.description: Sen. Darline Graham, who is serving out the remainder of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham's term in the Senate, said she will seek a full term for the South Carolina seat her brother held for more than two decades.

item.title: Trump to keep using old Air Force One while new plane gets security upgrades
item.description: The new Air Force One "has a lot of capability, but as I understand it, in about a month or so, they're going to send it to have it be maxed out," President Trump said Sunday.
